canonical_definition: A large-scale dataset of 3D articulated objects with part-level segmentation and kinematic information, used for training and evaluating models on articulation and reconstruction tasks.
papers: Articulate-Anything:  Automatic Modeling of Articulated Objects via a Vision-Language Foundation Model
Real2Code: Reconstruct Articulated Objects via Code Generation

definitions.text: A large-scale dataset of 3D articulated objects with part-level segmentation and kinematic information, used for training and evaluating models on articulation and reconstruction tasks.
definitions.paper: Real2Code: Reconstruct Articulated Objects via Code Generation

relationships.paper: Real2Code: Reconstruct Articulated Objects via Code Generation
relationships.evidence: For a more systematic evaluation, we validate the performance of Real2Code on the well-established PartNet-Mobility dataset (Mo et al., 2019), using an extensive test set of unseen objects that contain various numbers of articulated parts.
